mappings.rationaleAI: Directly addresses the Mongol Empire under Genghis Khan (C.1.a), successors (C.1.b), Yuan dynasty (C.2.a), and Golden Horde (C.2.d).
Trade routes connect to steppe peoples (A), Turkistan (B.3), Chagatai khanate (C.2.b), and Timurid Samarkand (C.2.c).
Tibetan Buddhist development addresses Tibet's Buddhism (D.1.a-c) and spread among Mongol peoples (C.2.e).
Vajrayana and Tibetan Buddhism connect to Tibet (D.1.a, D.1.b) and Mongolia (C.2.e).
Golden Horde's legacy connects to C.2.d. Russian expansion connects to waning nomad power (E).
Islam in Central Asia connects to Muslim rule in West Turkistan (B.3.a) and Afghanistan (B.5).
Iranian civilization connects to Afghanistan (B.5) and the Timurid dynasty (C.2.c).
Dynastic succession connects to Genghis Khan's division (C.1.b) and successor states (C.2).
Korean interaction connects to Manchurian tribes and their penetration into Korea (B.2).
